title: Postman Import/Export
description: Import Postman collection JSON files into yapi. May evolve to more first-class Postman support over time.

title: conf show: reflect db path overrides (--db flag / YAPI_DB env var)
description: When a --db CLI flag or YAPI_DB env var is added, update the conf show output to show the actual source of the db file path (e.g. 'set via --db', 'set via $YAPI_DB') in addition to the existing 'set in config.toml' and 'using default' sources.

title: conf show: reflect config path overrides (--config flag / YAPI_CONFIG env var)
description: When a --config CLI flag or YAPI_CONFIG env var is added, update the conf show output to show the actual source of the config file path (e.g. 'set via --config', 'set via $YAPI_CONFIG') instead of always 'using default'.

title: Debug Logging
description: Add structured debug/trace logging throughout the app using tracing or log crate. Instrument DB queries, request resolution, HTTP send, and config loading.

title: Allow create with json
description:
Allow creating items (e.g. request/collection/variable/env/etc. with json -- either passed in as a body or piped in to stdin.

The schema should roughly match the `--json` output from many commands to make for easy duplication. Aka:

```
yapi req show req1 --json | jq {{ some_transformation }} | yapi req create req2 --json -
```

Some fields like create/update timestamps and new IDs obviously should be ignored as they will need to be added on create.

Batch should also be possible if you're piping in an array but maybe that should be a different flag (since the names would have to be in the json not as an argument, etc.).

title: App struct: config + DB ownership and CLI dispatch
description: Create the App struct in src/app.rs that owns AppConfig and Database, provides App::new() for construction (load config, open DB) and App::run(cli) for CLI subcommand dispatch. main() becomes App::new()?.run(Cli::parse()).

title: Configuration
description: Set up layered configuration: $HOME/.config/yapi/config.toml (global) → .yapiconf.toml (directory-local) → CLI flags. Includes DB path resolution, config commands, and history retention settings.

title: Config file loading & types
description: Define AppConfig struct in src/conf.rs. Load from $HOME/.config/yapi/config.toml, merge with optional ./.yapiconf.toml for directory-local overrides. Layered: global → directory → flags.

title: DB path from config
description: Config specifies optional DB path, default $HOME/.config/yapi/yapi.db. Resolve DB location from config before opening connection.

title: yapi conf commands
description: yapi conf show/set/get for managing config values from the CLI.
close_reason: Implemented conf show/get/set commands with get_value/set_value functions and tests

title: History retention config
description: Config options for max history records, max age, and whether to store response bodies.

title: yapi conf init command
description: Add an init subcommand under yapi conf that creates a default config file at the global config path (~/.config/yapi/config.toml) if one doesn't exist.
close_reason: Implemented Init variant in ConfCmds and handler in run_conf

title: Add debug logging
description: Add tracing or log crate. Instrument key paths: DB queries, request resolution, HTTP send, config loading. Structured debug/trace output.

title: TUI
description: Terminal UI for interactive request building, browsing collections, and viewing history. Needs more design. Depends on CLI being functional first.

title: CLI
description: Wire up all CLI subcommands to actual functionality. Current main priority. Includes CRUD for workspaces, collections, environments, requests, plus request running, curl output, history, and variable evaluation.

title: curl-compatible flag parsing
description: Shared flag definitions for curl-compatible CLI flags: -X method, -H header, -d body, --data-urlencode query params. These will be reused across request creation and request running commands.
close_reason: Curl-compatible flags (-X, -H, -d, --query) already exist on ReqCreateArgs. Shared struct extraction can be done as part of yapi-vy0.11 if needed.

title: curl output mode
description: --dry-run / --as-curl flag to print the resolved request as a curl command instead of sending it.

title: Request override flags
description: Pass ad-hoc variables, override URL/headers/body/params at run time via CLI flags.

title: History commands
description: yapi hist list/show/del — browse and manage request history. Optionally print history entries as curl commands.

title: Fill out CLI subcommand definitions
description: Add sub-subcommands for coll, env, work, hist, conf, spec. Flesh out args/flags for each. This is the structural foundation all other CLI tasks depend on.

title: Workspace CRUD commands
description: yapi work list/create/show/update/del — full CRUD for workspaces via CLI.
close_reason: Implemented all 5 workspace CRUD handlers (list, create, show, update, del) with --force flag, --default-env option, empty-name validation, and tests

title: Collection CRUD commands
description: yapi coll list/create/show/update/del — full CRUD for collections via CLI.

title: Environment & variable CRUD commands
description: yapi env list/create/show/update/del, plus var sub-subcommands for managing variables within environments.

title: Request CRUD commands
description: yapi req list/create/show/update/del with curl-compatible flags (-X, -H, -d) for request creation. Depends on subcommand definitions and curl flag parsing.
close_reason: Request CRUD commands implemented and tested

title: Run requests
description: yapi req run {NAME} — resolve variables, send HTTP request, print response, save to history. Depends on request CRUD and config loading.

title: Variable listing & evaluation
description: yapi env vars list — show resolved variable map for a workspace+collection+env combo. Useful for debugging variable resolution.

title: Workspace/collection/env selection flags
description: --workspace, --collection, --env flags that override config defaults. Allows ad-hoc context selection without changing config.
close_reason: Already implemented. --workspace, --collection, and --env flags exist on all relevant subcommands.

title: Hooks & Test Assertions
description: Validate responses (status codes, body content, headers) without embedding a JS runtime like V8. Lower priority.
